element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic attack inputs against initial opponents
Player learns defensive positioning and directional evasion (two directions available)
Wide variety of punches available but limited movement constraints complicate execution
Opponents become more aggressive; limited dodging opportunity creates defensive pressure
Player must defeat opponents across three fictional boxing circuits to become undisputed heavyweight champion

typical_tension_triggers: Three knockdowns in single round risk (technical knockout condition)
Limited evasion options during opponent attacks
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Three-round matches with escalating pressure; tension builds through opponent aggression and visible damage accumulation; relief comes via knockdowns or round completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory opponents allow learning of basic punch and defense mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Opponents become more aggressive; limited character movement and dodging options create difficulty spikes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final opponents in three circuits present overwhelming difficulty; reviewers noted 'abysmal' and 'overwhelming' difficulty progression
notable_spikes: Mid-career aggression increase (limited dodge opportunity creates frustration)
Final championship bouts against stronger opponents
Cumulative damage from opponent pressure due to restricted movement

notable_level_patterns.description: Career mode requires defeating opponents across three fictional boxing circuits to unify heavyweight title belts; each circuit represents a progressive challenge tier

Each bout consists of exactly three rounds; victory conditions include knockout (4 knockdowns), technical knockout (3 knockdowns in single round), or judge's decision based on punches landed, knockdowns, and damage
Boxer portraits in 16-bit versions become progressively battered and bloody as fighters take damage; provides visual feedback on match state without numeric health bars
